attractions.name: American Tobacco Historic District (American Tobacco Campus) (Wikipedia, Discover Durham)
attractions.category: Landmark, Entertainment, Shopping, Restaurant (Discover Durham, Tripadvisor)
attractions.description: The American Tobacco Historic District is a revitalized factory complex that once served as the headquarters for the American Tobacco Company. Today, it is a vibrant multi-use campus featuring creative office spaces, restaurants, and entertainment venues set against a backdrop of beautifully restored industrial architecture (Discover Durham, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Management Office: Monday - Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Restaurants & Bars: Typically open until midnight; Campus grounds are generally accessible 24/7 (Greater Durham Chamber of Commerce, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Historic Lucky Strike water tower and brick chimney ([Wikipedia)', 'Man-made stream and relaxing outdoor green spaces (Tripadvisor)', "Burt's Bees Global Headquarters and observation hive (ATC Website)", 'The Cage, an outdoor basketball court and event space (ATC Website)', 'On-site restaurants and bars with patio seating (Discover Durham)', 'Proximity to the Durham Bulls Athletic Park (ATC Website)']
attractions.tips: Use the ParkMobile app for parking in the North, South, or East decks. Parking is often free on weekends unless there is a major event. Visit at dusk to see the iconic Lucky Strike tower illuminated (ATC Website).
attractions.nearby: Durham Bulls Athletic Park, Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC), Downtown Durham Main Street district (ATC Website)

attractions.name: Atomic Clock Brewing Company
attractions.description: Atomic Clock Brewing Company is a precision-focused brewery and taproom located in Durham's historic Warehouse District. Run by a sibling duo, the establishment features a diverse craft beer lineup, wine, cocktails, and a full-service kitchen led by an executive chef, offering both indoor and outdoor seating (Discover Durham, Durham Magazine).
attractions.hours: Monday: 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM (Discover Durham)
attractions.admission: Free (Downtown Durham Inc)
highlights: 'Full-service kitchen with unique menu items like Beef on Weck ([Discover Durham Reviews)', 'Located in the bustling Warehouse District near other craft breweries (Discover Durham)', 'Extensive beverage selection including IPAs, lagers, cocktails, and wine (Downtown Durham Inc)', 'Comfortable indoor and outdoor porch seating for all seasons (Discover Durham)', 'Named after the precision of atomic clocks to reflect their brewing philosophy (Durham Magazine)']
attractions.tips: The brewery provides its own on-site parking lot for visitors. The executive chef's kitchen is a major draw, so consider trying the food alongside your beer; the outdoor porch is particularly popular for its atmosphere (Skip the Small Talk, Discover Durham Reviews).
attractions.nearby: Durham Central Park, Durham Bulls Athletic Park, American Tobacco Campus, Museum of Durham History, Flying Bull Restaurant and Brewery (Discover Durham, Wayward Blog)

attractions.name: Bar Virgile Official Website
attractions.category: Bar, Restaurant Official Website, Discover Durham
attractions.description: Bar Virgile is a unique and sophisticated bar in downtown Durham known for its artfully crafted food and beverages. It offers an extensive spirits selection, including one of the largest in the Triangle, and specializes in both classic and innovative cocktails in a cozy, welcoming atmosphere Official Website, Discover Durham.

attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 10:00 PM); Tuesday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 10:00 PM); Wednesday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 10:00 PM); Thursday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 10:00 PM); Friday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 11:00 PM); Saturday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Kitchen until 11:00 PM); Sunday: Closed Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Official Website
highlights: 'Boasting one of the largest spirits selections in the Triangle, including whiskey, gin, rum, and agave [Official Website', 'Expertly crafted classic and innovative seasonal cocktails Official Website', 'Ever-changing dinner and small plates menu featuring locally sourced ingredients Discover Durham', 'Cozy atmosphere with leather banquettes and a bustling, hospitable vibe Official Website', 'Sister bar "Annexe" located in the basement for overflow seating and vinyl nights Discover Durham']
attractions.tips: Bar Virgile does not take reservations and operates on a first-come, first-served basis. It can get very crowded on DPAC show nights, so consider arriving early or visiting their sister bar, Annexe, for overflow seating. On Wednesday evenings, you can bring your own records to be played on the turntable Official Website, Discover Durham.
attractions.nearby: Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC), Carolina Theatre, American Tobacco Campus, Durham Bulls Athletic Park, The Pinhook Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Beer Durham
attractions.description: Beer Durham is a neighborhood bottle shop and growler filling station located in the heart of downtown Durham. It features 20 rotating craft beer taps, wine by the glass, and a curated selection of bottles and cans for on-site enjoyment or to-go. The shop also offers local North Carolina snacks in a welcoming, community-focused environment. (Discover Durham, Yelp)
attractions.hours: Wednesday-Thursday: 3:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Monday-Tuesday: Closed. (Beer Durham)
attractions.admission: Free admissions; beverages and snacks available for purchase. (Tripadvisor)
highlights: ['20 rotating craft beer taps featuring local and seasonal brews', 'Growler filling station for 32oz and 64oz fills', 'Wide selection of refrigerated and shelf-stable bottles and cans', 'Wine by the glass and local North Carolina snacks available', 'Dog-friendly environment with treats often available for furry visitors', 'Knowledgeable and friendly staff to assist with beer selections']
attractions.tips: The shop is located in the Measurement Building near the Durham Farmers Market parking lot and can be slightly difficult to find for first-time visitors. It is a smaller space with limited seating, making it ideal for a quick pint while shopping or grabbing beers to go. Check their website or social media for the most current tap list and upcoming 'Friday parties.' (Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Durham Central Park, Museum of Durham History, American Tobacco Historic District, Durham Bulls Athletic Park, The Parlour. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Bennett Place State Historic Site
attractions.description: Bennett Place is a historic site in Durham, North Carolina, that was the location of the largest troop surrender of the American Civil War in April 1865 NC Historic Sites. General Joseph E. Johnston surrendered the Southern armies of the Carolinas, Georgia, and Florida to Union General William T. Sherman at this simple farmhouse Wikipedia. Today, the site features reconstructed historic structures, a museum, and interpretive trails that explore the lives of the Bennett family and the war's end NCpedia.
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Closed Sunday, Monday, and designated state holidays NC Historic Sites.
attractions.admission: Admission is free; guided tours are $4 for adults (18-64), $3 for seniors (65+) and military, $1 for youth (3-17), and free for children (0-2) NC Historic Sites.
highlights: 'Site of the largest Confederate troop surrender of the Civil War [Discover Durham', 'Carefully reconstructed mid-19th century farmhouse, kitchen, and smokehouse NC Historic Sites', 'The Unity Monument, dedicated in 1923 to symbolize national reconciliation Sites@Duke', 'Visitor Center featuring a museum, research library, and "Dawn of Peace" audiovisual program Wikipedia', 'Annual living history events and surrender reenactments Museum of Durham History']
attractions.tips: Guided tours are typically offered at 11:00 AM, 1:00 PM, and 3:00 PM, weather and staffing permitting NC Historic Sites. Plan for about one hour for indoor exhibits and more time for the nature trails Coast2CoastWithKids. The site is partially wheelchair accessible with a 50-yard dirt pathway to the farm Discover Durham.
attractions.nearby: Duke University, Museum of Life + Science, Duke Homestead State Historic Site, West Point on the Eno, and Eno River State Park Discover Durham, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Black Wall Street / Historic Parrish Street
attractions.description: Parrish Street, famously known as "Black Wall Street," was a thriving center for African American entrepreneurship and financial services in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. This four-block district served as the headquarters for iconic institutions like North Carolina Mutual Life Insurance and Mechanics and Farmers Bank, earning Durham the nickname "Mecca of the Black South." Today, it is a preserved historic area featuring commemorative sculptures, public art, and markers that honor its legacy of economic empowerment and resilience (Discover Durham, Atlas Obscura).
attractions.hours: Public Street; accessible 24/7 (Discover Durham)
attractions.admission: Free (Discover Durham)
highlights: ['Mechanics and Farmers Bank (Historic 1921 Headquarters)', 'North Carolina Mutual Life Insurance Company (Former Site)', 'Six Commemorative Bronze Sculptures (Parrish Street Project)', 'Black Wall Street Gardens and Plaza (Public Art Spaces)', 'Ella West Gallery (Black-owned Fine Art Gallery)', 'Rogers Alley (Repurposed Historic Buildings)']
attractions.tips: Take a self-guided walking tour to follow the six historical markers that detail the area's rich history. Visit during daylight hours to fully appreciate the public art and sculptures, and stop by the nearby Discover Durham Visitor Info Center for a printed African American Heritage Guide (Discover Durham, Best of the Bull).
attractions.nearby: American Tobacco Campus, Durham Bulls Athletic Park, CCB Plaza (Major the Bull), Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC) (Discover Durham)

attractions.name: Brightleaf Square (Watts and Yuille Warehouses) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Brightleaf Square consists of two historic 1904 tobacco warehouses that have been meticulously repurposed into a premier destination for shopping, dining, and offices. The district preserves its industrial heritage with stunning Romanesque Revival architecture, featuring ornate red-brick masonry and a charming central courtyard. (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Tuesday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Wednesday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Thursday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Friday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Saturday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM, Sunday: 7:55 AM – 10:55 PM (Discover Durham)
attractions.admission: Free (Public shopping and dining district) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Historic 1904 Watts and Yuille tobacco warehouses ([Wikipedia)', 'Charming central brick courtyard for outdoor seating (Official Site)', 'Diverse dining options including Clouds Brewing and Emmy Squared Pizza (Official Site)', 'Unique boutiques and local retailers like Indio and Hamilton Hill (Official Site)', 'Integrated health services with One Medical onsite (Duke Health)']
attractions.tips: Visitors can enjoy 2 hours of complimentary parking in the lot across the street or by validating their parking at participating onsite businesses. It is a highly walkable area, well-suited for combining a shopping trip with a meal in the evening. (Reddit, Duke Health)
attractions.nearby: American Tobacco Historic District, Durham Bulls Athletic Park, Museum of Durham History, Carolina Theatre (Discover Durham)

attractions.name: Bull City Burger and Brewery (Bull City Burger and Brewery)
attractions.description: Bull City Burger and Brewery is a locally owned, independent restaurant and craft brewery that specializes in "farm-to-fork" dining using pasture-raised, grass-fed beef ground daily. It is the first certified B Corp restaurant in North Carolina and makes nearly all ingredients in-house, including buns, sauerkraut, and pickles. (Bull City Burger and Brewery, Tripadvisor)
attractions.address: 107 E. Parrish St. #105, Durham, NC 27701 (Bull City Burger and Brewery)
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:11 AM – 9:30 PM; Tuesday: 11:11 AM – 9:30 PM; Wednesday: 11:11 AM – 9:30 PM; Thursday: 11:11 AM – 9:30 PM; Friday: 11:11 AM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 11:11 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 11:11 AM – 9:00 PM (Bull City Burger and Brewery)
attractions.admission: Free (Bull City Burger and Brewery)
highlights: 'Pasture-raised, grass-fed beef burgers ground fresh daily ([Bull City Burger and Brewery)', 'Authentic, true-to-style craft beers brewed on-site (Bull City Burger and Brewery)', 'First Certified B Corp restaurant in North Carolina (Bull City Burger and Brewery)', 'Over 67 million possible hamburger combinations with 30+ toppings (Bull City Burger and Brewery)', 'Unique "Secret Burger" not listed on the public menu (Bull City Burger and Brewery)', 'Annual "Exotic Meat Month" featuring rare game meats each April (Bull City Burger and Brewery)']
attractions.tips: Sign up for the email list to receive a free birthday burger annually. Street parking is metered but becomes free after 7 PM and on weekends. Note that the restaurant has a strict "no outside food or drink" policy. (Bull City Burger and Brewery)
attractions.nearby: Pompieri Pizza, The Parlour Ice Cream, 21c Museum Hotel, Major Bull Statue, American Tobacco Campus, and Durham Bulls Athletic Park (Axios Charlotte)

attractions.name: Counting House
attractions.description: Counting House is a restaurant and bar located in the historic 21c Museum Hotel in downtown Durham, North Carolina. Led by Executive Chef Melanie Wilkerson, the establishment celebrates the rich culinary heritage of the Carolinas with a seafood-driven menu and playful execution, all set within a vibrant contemporary art museum environment. Counting House, 21c Museum Hotel
attractions.address: 111 North Corcoran Street, Durham, NC 27701 Counting House
attractions.hours: Brunch: Mon-Fri 7am-2pm, Sat-Sun 8am-2pm; Dinner: Sun-Thu 5pm-9pm, Fri-Sat 5pm-10pm; Lounge: Mon-Thu 11am-10pm, Fri-Sat 11am-12am; Lounge Snack Hour: Daily 2pm-5pm Counting House
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant/Bar within a free museum) 21c Museum Hotel
highlights: 'Integrated with the 21c Museum Hotel, featuring rotating 24/7 contemporary art galleries [Counting House', 'Regional seafood-focused menu curated by Durham native Executive Chef Melanie Wilkerson Counting House', 'Housed in the historic Hill Building, featuring original terrazzo floors and Art Deco details Counting House', 'Features "The Vault Society," a unique lounge experience set in the building\'s original bank vault Counting House', 'Home to the iconic 21c pink penguins found throughout the dining and lounge areas Yelp', 'Award-winning cocktail program recognized as one of the best in the Triangle region Indy Week']
attractions.tips: Explore the 21c Museum's art galleries before or after your meal, as they are free and open 24/7 21c Museum Hotel. Visit the basement to see the original bank vault and experience the unique lounge atmosphere, and don't miss the "See-Thru Bathrooms" for a notable architectural curiosity Yelp. Reservations are highly recommended for dinner, particularly during show nights at the nearby DPAC Tripadvisor.
attractions.nearby: Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC) Tripadvisor, Carolina Theatre Cottages at Brightleaf, Museum of Durham History Cottages at Brightleaf, Durham Bulls Athletic Park Cottages at Brightleaf

attractions.name: Crank Arm Brewing Company Crank Arm Brewing
attractions.description: Crank Arm Brewing is a bike-themed craft brewery and taproom located in the warehouse district of downtown Durham. It focuses on cultivating an active community through its "Beer Love, Bike Love" philosophy, offering award-winning unfiltered beers, local cider, NC wine, and a full spirit bar. Crank Arm Brewing, Discover Durham
attractions.address: 620 Fernway Drive, Durham, NC 27701 Crank Arm Brewing
attractions.hours: Monday: 2:00 pm – 10:00 pm; Tuesday: 2:00 pm – 10:00 pm; Wednesday: 2:00 pm – 12:00 am; Thursday: 2:00 pm – 12:00 am; Friday: 12:00 pm – 12:00 am; Saturday: 12:00 pm – 12:00 am; Sunday: 12:00 pm – 10:00 pm Crank Arm Brewing
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Crank Arm Brewing
attractions.admission: Free entry; beer and beverages for purchase Expedia
highlights: 'Bike-themed decor featuring cycling memorabilia and industrial-chic design [This Is Raleigh', 'Weekly "cRUNk" Run Club and community bicycle rides Meetup', 'Diverse drink selection including unfiltered craft beers, cocktails, and non-alcoholic options Crank Arm Brewing', 'Spacious taproom located on the ground floor of the Beckon Building Crank Arm Brewing', 'Regular community events like Craft & Draft Tuesdays and trivia nights Meetup', 'Dog-friendly atmosphere with an active, welcoming vibe Discover Durham']
attractions.tips: Join the run club on Mondays for free chips and salsa from the neighboring Torero's. The brewery is dog-friendly and encourages an active lifestyle, so feel free to bring your bike or pup. Meetup, Discover Durham
attractions.nearby: Torero's Mexican Restaurant, Durham Central Park, Morris Green Park, Atomic Clock Brewing Co., Flying Bull Restaurant and Brewery Meetup, Discover Durham

attractions.name: DSSOLVR Durham
attractions.description: DSSOLVR Durham is a surreal and vibrant taproom located in the heart of Durham's Central Park District, known for its diverse selection of craft beers and psychedelic, alien-themed decor. As an extension of the popular Asheville brewery, it offers an inclusive and dog-friendly space where visitors can enjoy everything from lagers and IPAs to hard seltzers and cocktails. The venue frequently hosts community events such as trivia, poker, and craft nights, making it a dynamic hub for locals and visitors alike. Official Website, Discover Durham
attractions.hours: Monday – Thursday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (Specific events may have ticket prices; e.g., Dungeons n' Drafts for $20) Discover Durham, Official Website
highlights: ['Diverse craft beer selection including lagers, IPAs, sours, and wood-fermented varieties', 'Unique psychedelic, alien-themed artwork and murals throughout the taproom', 'Dog-friendly environment that allows pets indoors and provides water bowls and toys', 'Weekly event lineup featuring trivia, poker Sundays, and Magic: The Gathering nights', 'Participation in "The Bullpen" social district, allowing to-go alcoholic beverages in the area', 'Offerings beyond beer, including mixed drinks, cider, and non-alcoholic options']
attractions.tips: Visit on Tuesdays for $6 IPA specials and trivia night, or Sundays for $2 off flights and mimosas. Since the taproom does not have an in-house kitchen, guests are encouraged to bring food from nearby eateries like the Durham Food Hall. The space is exceptionally pet-friendly, so feel free to bring your dog inside while you enjoy a drink. Official Website, Yelp
attractions.nearby: Durham Central Park, Historic Durham Athletic Park, Durham Farmers' Market, Motorco Music Hall, Fullsteam Brewery

It features a cozy downstairs ramen shop specializing in exquisite noodle bowls and an upstairs izakaya (Japanese pub) offering innovative small plates and an extensive selection of Japanese spirits. Dashi Official Website, Discover Durham

attractions.tips: Two parking decks are located within a block (Morgan Rigsbee Garage and others); on-street parking is also available. Reservations are recommended for the izakaya and private omakase sessions. Check their Instagram for weekly specials and featured drinks. Dashi Official Website

attractions.name: Dos Perros Restaurant
attractions.description: Dos Perros was a refined yet casual Mexican restaurant in downtown Durham that specialized in authentic, upscale cuisine rather than standard Tex-Mex. Founded in 2009 by chef Charlie Deal, it became a staple of the city's dining revival, known for its extensive tequila list and inclusive menu featuring numerous vegan and gluten-free options. (News & Observer, Revista Latina)
attractions.address: 200 N Mangum St, Suite 101, Durham, NC 27701
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 5:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Sunday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Brunch: Saturday & Sunday 11:30 AM - 2:30 PM (Legacy schedule) (Morehead Manor, Triangle Food Blog)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Legacy schedule prior to 2022 closure) (Morehead Manor)

(Yelp)', 'Strong focus on dietary inclusivity with a wide range of vegan and gluten-free dishes. (ABC11)', 'Outdoor patio seating offering prime people-watching in the heart of downtown Durham. (Yelp)', "Named after the owner's two beloved rescued golden retrievers, Nikki and Alex. (Revista Latina)"]
attractions.tips: It was a popular choice for pre-theater dining due to its proximity to DPAC. Fans highly recommended trying the plantain rellenos and the signature habanero salsa for those who enjoy significant heat. (Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC), Carolina Theatre, American Tobacco Campus, 21c Museum Hotel (Discover Durham, Yelp)

attractions.name: Duke Forest (Teaching and Research Laboratory)
attractions.description: Duke Forest is a 7,000-acre "living laboratory" owned and managed by Duke University for research, teaching, and limited public recreation. Established in 1931, it spans Durham, Orange, and Alamance counties, providing a diverse natural landscape for academic study and outdoor activities like hiking and biking. (Duke Forest)
attractions.address: 308 Research Drive, Levine Science Research Center Suite A233 (Office); NC 751 (Shepherd Nature Trail)
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset; Friday: Sunrise - Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset (Duke Forest)
attractions.admission: Free (Duke Forest)
highlights: ['Shepherd Nature Trail: A self-guided educational loop with informative signage.', 'Extensive Road Network: Miles of graded and graveled "fire roads" ideal for running and hiking.', 'Diverse Ecosystems: Home to various forest stands, open fields, and the scenic New Hope Creek.', 'Research Infrastructure: A site for numerous long-term scientific studies and teaching projects.', 'Wildlife Viewing: Opportunities for birdwatching and observing local flora and fauna.']
attractions.tips: Dogs must be kept on a leash at all times; recreation is restricted to forest roads and designated trails; do not block gates when parking; no off-trail activity or recreation after sunset. (Duke Forest)
attractions.nearby: Duke Lemur Center, Sarah P. Duke Gardens, Nasher Museum of Art, Eno River State Park (Summer Session - Duke University)

attractions.name: Duke University East Campus
attractions.description: Duke University's East Campus is a historic 97-acre site that serves as the exclusive residential home for all first-year undergraduate students. Characterized by its elegant Georgian-style red brick architecture, the campus provides a close-knit community environment with its own library, dining hall, and recreational facilities. It was the original site of Trinity College in Durham before the expansion of West Campus in the 1930s. Wikipedia, Duke Student Affairs
attractions.hours: Campus grounds: Open 24/7; Lilly Library: Mon-Thu 8:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Fri 8:00 AM – 9:00 AM, Sat 10:00 AM – 9:00 AM, Sun 2:00 PM – 2:00 AM Wikipedia, Duke Arts
attractions.admission: Free (Public areas and walking path are free; tuition applies for enrolled students) Duke Bursar, Discover Durham
highlights: ['Georgian-style architecture distinct from the Gothic West Campus', 'Lilly Library, the primary research hub for first-year students', 'The Marketplace, the main dining facility for freshmen', 'Brodie Recreation Center featuring courts, weights, and saunas', 'Baldwin Auditorium, a premier venue for musical performances', 'The East Campus Loop, a popular 1.7-mile public walking and running trail']
attractions.tips: The 1.7-mile perimeter path is excellent for walking or jogging and is very popular with locals. Visitors should use the Duke Explore app for a self-guided history tour of the campus. Public parking is limited on weekdays; consider visiting on weekends or using the C1 bus if you are affiliated with the university. Duke Admissions, Discover Durham
attractions.nearby: Ninth Street District, Trinity Park, Brightleaf Square, Sarah P. Duke Gardens, Nasher Museum of Art Discover Durham, Duke Student Affairs

attractions.name: Durham Bulls Athletic Park (DBAP)
attractions.description: Durham Bulls Athletic Park (DBAP) is a premier 10,000-seat minor league ballpark that serves as the home for the Triple-A Durham Bulls. Designed with a classic "old-time" aesthetic, it features unique elements like the 32-foot-high "Blue Monster" wall and the famous snorting bull sign from the movie *Bull Durham* (MiLB.com, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Gamedays (Mon-Fri) from 9:00 AM, (Sat-Sun) from 12:00 PM; Non-Gamedays (Mon-Fri) 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Ballpark Corner Store: Mon-Fri 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sat 12:00 PM – 3:00 PM (MiLB.com).
attractions.days_open: Open daily for events during the baseball season (typically April–September); the Box Office and Team Store are open year-round, Monday through Friday (MiLB.com).
attractions.admission: Single game tickets typically range from $13 to $48 depending on the opponent and section; group tickets are available for $10 to $30 per person (MiLB.com, Vivid Seats). Premium options like the 8-person and 12-person boxes range from $215 to $344 (MiLB.com).
highlights: 'The iconic "Blue Monster" left-field wall featuring a manual scoreboard ([MiLB.com).', 'The "Snorting Bull" sign that blows smoke and wags its tail after a Bulls home run (MiLB.com).', 'Wool E. World, a large Chick-fil-A sponsored playground for children (MiLB.com).', 'A 360-degree walkable concourse providing uninterrupted views of the field (MiLB.com).', "Jackie's Landing, a 10,000-square-foot social space with the 42 Bar (MiLB.com).", 'The upscale PNC Triangle Club for premium ticket holders (MiLB.com).']
attractions.tips: The ballpark is a completely cashless facility; only credit and debit cards are accepted for food, beverages, and merchandise (MiLB.com). Tickets are fully digital and should be managed through the MiLB First Pitch app (MiLB.com). Arrive early to explore the surrounding American Tobacco Campus for pre-game dining (Durham Sports Commission).
attractions.nearby: American Tobacco Historic District, Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC), Museum of Durham History, Bull City Ciderworks, and Sarah P. Duke Gardens (TripAdvisor).

attractions.name: Durham Central Park
attractions.description: Durham Central Park is a five-acre city-owned green space located in the heart of Downtown Durham, managed by a non-profit organization. This vibrant urban oasis features public art, natural beauty, community events, and recreational facilities, serving as the central hub for the local community and a premier destination for cultural activities. (Discover Durham, Durham Central Park)
attractions.hours: Dawn - Dusk (Every day) (Durham Central Park)
attractions.admission: Free (Durham Central Park)
highlights: "Durham Farmers' Market held weekly at the 9,000+ sq. ft. Pavilion ([Durham Central Park)", "Mount Merrill interactive children's play area with slides and climbing nets (Durham Central Park)", '10,000 sq. ft. custom concrete Skate Park designed by Artisan Skateparks (Durham Central Park)', 'Numerous public art installations including the Magnolia Mural and the Pixel Wall (Durham Central Park, Discover Durham)', "The Garden of Eatin', a free-pick public community garden (Durham Central Park)", 'The Leaf, a unique outdoor performance and seating structure (Durham Central Park)']
attractions.tips: Visit on Saturday mornings to experience the bustling Durham Farmers' Market and Craft Market. Parking is available on-site with hourly rates, but often has a $5 maximum on weekends. (Durham Central Park, Durham Magazine)
attractions.nearby: Durham Farmers' Market, Fullsteam Brewery, Motorco Music Hall, Cocoa Cinnamon, Little Barb's Bakery, Durham Food Hall (Durham Magazine, WRAL)

attractions.name: Durham Distillery
attractions.description: Founded in 2013, Durham Distillery is a pioneer in craft distillation located in the heart of downtown Durham, North Carolina. Known for its award-winning Conniption Gin, the distillery combines traditional methods with modern chemistry and cold vacuum distillation to create exceptional spirits and liqueurs.
attractions.hours: Tasting Room (Retail & Bottle Sales): Sun 2 PM - 4 PM; Mon - Thu 10 AM - 5 PM (Ring Doorbell); Fri - Sat 12 PM - 7 PM. Conniption Bar & Lounge: Wed - Sat 4 PM - 11 PM; Sun 4 PM - 9 PM.
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Sunday (Tasting Room also open Monday - Thursday for retail)
attractions.admission: Behind the Scenes Tour: $20.00 (includes guided walk, tasting flight, and souvenir shot glass). General entry to Tasting Room and Bar/Lounge is free; individual drinks and retail items are for purchase.
highlights: ['Birthplace of the award-winning Conniption Gin, the most awarded American gin line.', 'Features the Conniption Bar & Lounge (formerly Corpse Reviver) for expertly crafted cocktails.', "Offers 'Behind the Scenes' tours and guided tastings of their diverse spirit portfolio.", 'Utilizes a custom-designed German copper pot still and innovative vacuum cold distillation.', 'Housed in a lovingly restored former auto body shop and coffin manufacturer building.', 'Woman-led business founded by scientist team Melissa and Lee Katrincic.']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for the $20 guided tours as they frequently sell out. Visitors must be 21 or older to attend tours. If visiting the Tasting Room during the week (Mon-Thu), you may need to ring the doorbell for entry. Don't miss the seasonal 'Halloween PopUp' event at the Conniption Bar & Lounge.
attractions.nearby: Motorco Music Hall, Geer Street Garden, Duke Homestead, Fullsteam Brewery, and the Central Park district.

attractions.name: Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC)
attractions.description: Since its opening in 2008, DPAC has become the center for live entertainment in North Carolina. It features 2,704 seats with intimate sightlines and state-of-the-art sound and video, hosting over 200 performances annually including touring Broadway productions and high-profile concerts (Broadway.org, DPAC About Us).

attractions.name: Duke University Chapel
attractions.description: Duke University Chapel is a magnificent Collegiate Gothic landmark located at the heart of Duke University's West Campus. Completed in 1935 and designed by the pioneering African-American architect Julian Abele, it serves as an ecumenical Christian center for worship, music, and university ceremonies. The structure is renowned for its 210-foot tower, intricate stone carvings, and vibrant stained-glass windows, making it one of the most iconic buildings in North Carolina (Wikipedia, Official Site).

Duke (Official Site).', 'Intricate woodwork and limestone carvings throughout the sanctuary and choir (NCPedia).', 'Regular Choral Evensong and Sunday morning worship services featuring the Duke Chapel Choir (Official Site).']
attractions.tips: Visitors should check the online calendar before arriving, as the chapel frequently closes for weddings, funerals, and university events. Public parking is available for a fee at the nearby Bryan Center Parking Garage (125 Science Drive). Docent-led tours are typically offered on Sundays following the 11:00 a.m. worship service (Official Site).

It features three large gallery spaces showcasing a permanent collection strong in Medieval art, art of the Americas, Classical Antiquities, and modern and contemporary art, alongside rotating international exhibitions. The restaurant features a warm atmosphere with red accent walls, black and white celebrity photos, and traditional white table cloths, offering a menu focused on Provençal French classics like steak frites and fruits de mer.

attractions.name: West Point on the Eno (Durham Parks & Recreation)
attractions.description: West Point on the Eno is a 404-acre natural and historic city park located along a scenic two-mile stretch of the Eno River in Durham, North Carolina (Durham Parks & Recreation). It offers a unique combination of outdoor recreation and historical preservation, featuring a reconstructed grist mill, a photography museum, and a mid-19th-century farmhouse (Wikipedia). Visitors can enjoy five miles of hiking trails, picnicking, fishing, and paddling in a serene river setting that has remained largely unchanged for centuries (Eno River Association).
attractions.address: 5101 N Roxboro St, Durham, NC 27704 (Durham Parks & Recreation)
attractions.hours: Park Gates: Monday-Sunday 8:00 AM - Dusk; McCown-Mangum House & Hugh Mangum Museum of Photography: Saturday-Sunday 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM (mid-January to mid-December); West Point Mill is currently closed until further notice (Durham Parks & Recreation)
attractions.admission: Free for all visitors (Eno River Association)
highlights: 'Historic West Point Grist Mill, a reproduction colonial-era mill ([Wikipedia)', 'McCown-Mangum House, a restored 1850s farmhouse (Durham Parks & Recreation)', 'Hugh Mangum Museum of Photography, featuring historic 19th-century photography equipment and negatives (Eno River Association)', 'Five miles of scenic hiking trails, including sections of the Mountains-to-Sea Trail (Tripadvisor)', 'Canoe and kayak access to the scenic Eno River (Discover Durham)', 'Natural Play Space for children and picnic facilities (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy shoes for the rocky river trails and bring a picnic to enjoy by the water (Tripadvisor). Building tours are limited to weekend afternoons, so plan your visit accordingly (Durham Parks & Recreation).
